architecture.summary: Debate on Graph (DoG) is an iterative interactive framework that integrates LLMs with knowledge graphs for KGQA. Information flows as follows: the system employs a multi-role debate team (debater roles) that interactively reason over a knowledge graph, using a subgraph-focusing mechanism to narrow the search space after each reasoning step. At each iteration, the LLM attempts to generate an answer based on the current subgraph, then refines its reasoning by debating candidate relations and paths, gradually simplifying complex questions while filtering false-positive relations. The framework alternates between graph traversal, answer generation, and debate-driven path refinement until convergence.

biblio.title: Debate on Graph: A Flexible and Reliable Reasoning Framework for Large Language Models
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: DoG outperforms the state-of-the-art method ToG by 23.7% in accuracy on WebQuestions
DoG outperforms the state-of-the-art method ToG by 9.1% in accuracy on GrailQA
DoG employs a subgraph-focusing mechanism, allowing LLMs to perform answer trying after each reasoning step, thereby mitigating the impact of lengthy reasoning paths
DoG utilizes a multi-role debate team to gradually simplify complex questions, reducing the influence of false-positive relations
Experimental results on five public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our architecture
Integration experiments with various LLMs on the mentioned datasets highlight the flexibility of DoG
